headline: Why Today’s Small Dividend Could Become Tomorrow’s Retirement Engine
summary: A stock screener sorted by current yield misses one of the most powerful income stories in the market. Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) now pays $0.91 per quarter, up from $0.08 per quarter in 2005. Visa (NYSE: V) most recently paid $0.67 per quarter, and its annual dividend now totals $2.68. Those stocks do not look like ... Why Today’s Small Dividend Could Become Tomorrow’s Retirement Engine

headline: 6% APY, Unlimited 3% Cash Back and Free ATM Withdrawals: Everything You Need to Know About Elon Musk's 'Bank Killer' X Money App
summary: Elon Musk is trying to turn X into a one-stop shop for all your financial needs. Win or lose, X Money is showing us a clear direction of travel for consumer finance.

headline: Visa (V) Expands Vietnam Payments Reach With Direct 9Pay Partnership
summary: Visa (NYSE:V) has entered a direct partnership with Vietnamese payment provider 9Pay, enabling seamless international card payments in Vietnam. The move allows multinational firms to accept Visa payments in Vietnam without holding local licenses, using 9Pay’s all in one payment solution. 9Pay has received global recognition for its payment platform, which combines technical integration, security standards and compliance features. For investors watching Visa, this new link with 9Pay is about...
